rulings.Case Title: EMILIANO SUPIPING VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on plaintiff's factually devoid discovery. Plaintiff fails to present admissible evidence that plaintiff was exposed to asbestos as a consequence of defendant's acts. The Court strikes the Cohen Declaration for lack of foundation demonstrating that the reentrainment theory is generally accepted in the scientific community. Even if admitted, plaintiff's evidence is speculative that any of the work done by defendant caused harm to plaintiff. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIRAUDO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Order Granting Leave To File 1st Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 2. PLAINTIFF JOHN GIRAUDO'S MOTION For Order Granting Leave To File 1st Amended Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL R BARNES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Appoint Elizabeth Barnes As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Pltf And For Leave To File 1st Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 3. PLAINTIFF PAUL BARNES' MOTION To Appoint Elizabeth Barnes As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File 1st Amended Complaint is granted. Defendant's objections may be technically correct but better purpose would be served by requiring plaintiff to refile the request. Defendant may point out the differences in the original and amended complaint in the course of this case. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA ARRIAGA VS. SUPREME CHICKEN RC, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANTS SUPREME CHICKEN RC, LLC, FERNANDO BORJA, SUPREME CHICKEN OF OAKLAND, LLC, ET AL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled, 10 days to answer. The complaint pleads sufficient facts to state causes of action for breach of contract, fraud and alter ego. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE LIN VS. ZEN COMPOUND, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS ZEN COMPOUND, LLC, PAUL HEMMING, WENDY HEMMING, ET AL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend only because plaintiff now has obtained counsel. The amended complaint must identify, with specificity or attachment, the terms and parties of any agreement at issue. Plaintiff must set forth facts that allege a basis to find that the deed in lieu of foreclosure is not controlling. The amended complaint must set forth the events at issue in chronological order and provide facts that demonstrate that the claims are not barred by the statute of limitations, the statute of frauds and/or laches. The wrongful acts of each defendant must be delineated. Facts must be plead to establish a duty of care owed to plaintiff by Jeffrey Hammond. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JAYPAR, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION VS. POSITIVEEDGESOLUTIONS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jaypar, Inc'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 11. PLAINTIFF JAYPAR, INC'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. The motion is denied. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate probable irreparrable harm. It appears that plaintiff was on notice of a possible claim on June 8, and waited until August 22, to file the complaint and waited nearly two more months to seek injunctive relief. Probability of success is uncertain. The covenants not to compete are not enforceable under California law and plaintiff has failed to identify with any specificity a trade secret allegedly taken; rather, plaintiff's complaint and declaration are replete with generaltities and the mere identify of a customer already disclosed on plaintiff's web site. The equities favor defendants for any injunction would likely put defendants out of business. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JAYPAR, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION VS. POSITIVEEDGESOLUTIONS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANTS SANTANU MONDAL, ASMITA KATDARE AND SHIVENDRA SAINGER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained; 10 days leave to amend as to certain cause of action. California has declared unlawful the noncompetition clause central to plaintiff's claim. Application Group Inc. v. Hunter Group, Inc. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 881, 902; Kolani v. Gluska (1998) 64 Cal.App. 4th 402. Leave to amend is granted as to the first cause of action to plead a cause of action under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act, Cal. Civil Code section 3426.1 et. seq. Plaintiff's claim based on Maryland law fails. Leave to amend is denied as to the second (breach of confidentiality agreement), third (breach of covenant not to complete), 4th(breach of covenant not to solicit) and 13th (declaratory relief) causes of action. Business & Professions Code section 16600 renders the clause at issue unlawful. Leave to amend is granted as to the fifth (unfair competition), eighth (intentional interference with contract), tenth (intentional interference with prospective economic advantage) and the eleventh (negilgent interference with prospective economic advantage) causes of action for plaintiff to plead conduct unrelated to misappropriation of trade secrets. The decisions in Callaway Golf v. Dunlap Slazenger Group Americas, Inc. (D. Del. 2004) 318 F.Supp. 216 and Digital Envoy, Inc. v. Google Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2005) 370 F.Supp. 1025 make clear that the facts alleged are preempted by the Uniform Trade Secrets Act cause of action. The complaint appears to reflect that plaintiff may be able to allege other facts apart from the misappropriation claim. Leave to amend is denied as to the ninth cause of action. The decision in Fifield v. Finston (1960) 54 Cal.2d 632 bars the claim. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JAYPAR, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION VS. POSITIVEEDGESOLUTIONS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 30, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT POSITIVEEDGE SOLUTIONS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained; 10 days leave to amend as to certain causes of action. Leave to amend is granted as to the first cause of action to plead a cause of action under the Uniform Trade Secrets Act. Leave to amend is denied as to the second and thirteenth causes of action. Positiveedge is not a party to the contract and the provision at issue is unlawful under Business & Professions Code section 16600. Leave to amend is granted as to the fifth, eighth, tenth and eleventh causes of action to plead conduct unrelated to the misappropriation claim. Plaintiff also must plead more specific facts to clarify the Servicesoft contract. Leave to amend is denied as to the ninth cause of action. =(302/PJM/pb)
